User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account within the LocalBid Exchange app, including their profile information and aggregated statistics as a seller.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The public display name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user. Used for identification, not direct authentication storage.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

locationCoords.description: Geographical coordinates representing the user's general location, used for item discovery and meeting suggestions.

isIdVerified.description: Indicates if the user has completed the ID verification process to earn a 'Verified' badge.

totalSalesMade.description: Aggregated count of successful sales made by this user as a seller.

averageSellerRating.description: Aggregated average rating received by this user as a seller from buyer reviews.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an item listed for sale on the LocalBid Exchange app, either as a direct sale or an auction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sellerId.description: Reference to the User who is selling this product. (Relationship: User 1:N Product)

title.description: The title of the product listing.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The fixed price for a direct sale item. Null if the product is part of an auction.

category.description: The category the product belongs to (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Clothing', 'Home Goods').

images.description: An array of URLs to images of the product.

condition.description: The condition of the product (e.g., 'New', 'Used - Like New', 'Used - Good').

locationCoords.description: Geographical coordinates representing the general location of the product, used for proximity searches.

status.description: The current status of the product listing (e.g., 'available', 'sold', 'auction_active', 'auction_ended', 'removed').

isBoosted.description: Indicates if the listing has been paid to be boosted for increased visibility.

auctionId.description: Reference to the Auction entity if this product is being sold via auction. (Relationship: Product 1:1 Auction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product listing was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product listing was last updated.

Auction.title: Auction
Auction.description: Represents the details of an auction for a specific product, allowing users to place bids over a period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Auction entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product being sold in this auction. (Relationship: Product 1:1 Auction)

startingBid.description: The initial minimum bid amount for the auction.

currentHighestBid.description: The current highest bid placed on the auction.

highestBidderId.description: Reference to the User who placed the current highest bid. Null if no bids have been placed. (Relationship: User 1:N Bid)

startTime.description: The date and time when the auction officially started.

endTime.description: The date and time when the auction is scheduled to end.

status.description: The current status of the auction (e.g., 'scheduled', 'active', 'ended', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the auction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the auction record was last updated.

Bid.title: Bid
Bid.description: Represents a single bid placed by a user on an auction item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bid entity.

auctionId.description: Reference to the Auction this bid is for. (Relationship: Auction 1:N Bid)

bidderId.description: Reference to the User who placed this bid. (Relationship: User 1:N Bid)

bidTime.description: Timestamp when the bid was placed.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a completed or in-progress purchase of a product, including commission and meeting details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product involved in this transaction. (Relationship: Product 1:1 Transaction)

buyerId.description: Reference to the User who bought the product. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

sellerId.description: Reference to the User who sold the product. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

finalPrice.description: The total amount paid by the buyer, including the commission.

commissionAmount.description: The commission amount deducted from the seller's payout or added to the buyer's cost, typically 8-10%.

commissionRate.description: The percentage rate of commission applied to this transaction (e.g., 0.08 for 8%).

transactionDate.description: Timestamp when the transaction was completed or confirmed.

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'pending_payment', 'paid_escrow', 'funds_released', 'completed', 'cancelled').

meetingPointAddress.description: A descriptive address or name of the agreed-upon meeting point for item exchange.

meetingPointCoords.description: Geographical coordinates for the agreed-upon meeting point.

suggestedMeetingPointBy.description: Indicates who suggested the meeting point (e.g., 'buyer', 'seller', 'system').

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: Reference ID from the external payment gateway for this transaction.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a buyer's review and rating given to a seller after a transaction.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

transactionId.description: Reference to the Transaction this review is associated with. (Relationship: Transaction 1:N Review)

sellerId.description: Reference to the User who is being reviewed (the seller). (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

reviewerId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the review (the buyer). (Relationship: User 1:N Review)

rating.description: The rating given to the seller (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: The textual comment provided by the reviewer.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review was created.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents an in-app message exchanged between users, potentially related to a product listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Identifier for the conversation thread this message belongs to. This helps group messages between two users about a specific context.

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

receiverId.description: Reference to the User who received the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

productId.description: Optional reference to the Product the message is about. (Relationship: Product 1:N Message)

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Indicates if the receiver has read the message.

isSuspicious.description: Flag indicating if the message was flagged as suspicious by the anti-scam AI filter.

suspicionReason.description: Optional description of why the message was flagged as suspicious (e.g., 'contains suspicious link').

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report filed by a user against another user or a product listing for suspicious or inappropriate content.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Report entity.

reporterId.description: Reference to the User who filed the report. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

reportedUserId.description: Optional reference to the User who is being reported. (Relationship: User 1:N Report)

reportedProductId.description: Optional reference to the Product listing that is being reported. (Relationship: Product 1:N Report)

reportType.description: The type of issue being reported (e.g., 'scam', 'inappropriate_content', 'spam', 'fraudulent_listing').

description.description: A detailed description of the reported issue.

status.description: The current status of the report (e.g., 'pending', 'reviewed', 'resolved', 'dismissed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the report was filed.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the report was last updated.

BlockedUser.title: BlockedUser
BlockedUser.description: Represents a record of one user blocking another user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlockedUser relationship entity.

blockerId.description: Reference to the User who initiated the block. (Relationship: User 1:N BlockedUser)

blockedId.description: Reference to the User who has been blocked. (Relationship: User 1:N BlockedUser)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the block was initiated.

ListingBoost.title: ListingBoost
ListingBoost.description: Represents a paid boost applied to a product listing to increase its visibility and reach.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ListingBoost entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product listing that is being boosted. (Relationship: Product 1:N ListingBoost)

boosterId.description: Reference to the User (seller) who paid for this boost. (Relationship: User 1:N ListingBoost)

durationHours.description: The duration of the boost in hours.

startTime.description: The date and time when the boost became active.

endTime.description: The date and time when the boost is scheduled to expire.

cost.description: The monetary cost of applying this boost.

status.description: The current status of the boost (e.g., 'active', 'expired', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the boost record was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Access is restricted to the user themselves for modification, but public for viewing general profile details. The 'id' field matches the {userId} wildcard for path-based authorization.

Stores details for product listings available for sale. These documents are publicly readable for discovery. Write access is restricted to the 'sellerId' specified within the document, ensuring Authorization Independence without hierarchical dependencies.

Stores details for auction listings. These are publicly readable. Write access (e.g., updating status, end time) is restricted to the 'sellerId' of the associated product, which must be denormalized into this document as 'sellerId' for Authorization Independence. Includes denormalized 'sellerId' for authorization independence.

Stores individual bids placed on an auction. Readable by the 'bidderId' (from the document) and the 'sellerId' (denormalized from the parent Auction document) to ensure Authorization Independence for bid visibility. Bids are typically immutable after creation. Includes denormalized 'sellerId' (of the auction) for authorization independence.

params.name: auctionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Auction.

params.name: bidId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bid.

definition.description: Stores details of completed or in-progress product purchases. Access is restricted to the 'buyerId' or 'sellerId' within the document, enabling Authorization Independence without hierarchical lookups.

Stores buyer reviews for sellers. Reviews are publicly readable (for seller profiles) but write/update access is restricted to the 'reviewerId' within the document. The 'sellerId' in the path facilitates querying all reviews for a specific seller.

params.name: sellerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who is being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Review.

definition.description: Represents a unique conversation thread between users. This document primarily exists to group messages and stores key metadata like 'participantIds' (an array of user UIDs) to grant read/write access to all messages within this conversation to its participants. Note: this is an implicit 'Conversation' entity, reusing the 'Message' schema for its relationship, but logically represents the conversation metadata.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the conversation thread.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a conversation. Access is restricted to the 'senderId' or 'receiverId' within the document, ensuring Authorization Independence for message access without requiring a get() on the parent Conversation document.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Message.

definition.description: Stores reports filed by users. Access is restricted to the 'reporterId' within the document for the creator, and administrators (via DBAC pattern) for review and resolution.
Records users blocked by another user. Access is strictly controlled by the 'blockerId' in the path, ensuring only the blocking user can manage their blocked list.

params.name: blockerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who initiated the block.

params.name: blockedId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who has been blocked.

definition.description: Stores details about paid listing boosts for products. Access is restricted to the 'boosterId' (which is the seller) within the document, ensuring Authorization Independence for managing boosts. The 'productId' in the path links boosts directly to their respective product.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Product listing.

params.name: boostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ListingBoost.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for LocalBid Exchange prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust security rules and efficient queries. 

**Authorization Independence:**
Critical authorization contexts are denormalized into the documents themselves, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules for authorization checks. For example:
*   The `Product` document directly contains `sellerId`, allowing rules to check `resource.data.sellerId == request.auth.uid` for ownership without querying the user's profile or parent collection.
*   `Auction` documents will include a denormalized `sellerId` (derived from the associated product's seller) to allow the seller to manage their auctions directly. Similarly, `Bid` documents will denormalize the `sellerId` of the auction, enabling sellers to view bids on their auctions without a `get()` to the parent `Auction` document.
*   `Transaction` documents include both `buyerId` and `sellerId`, enabling both parties to access their respective transactions independently.
*   For `Message` entities, the `senderId` and `receiverId` are directly within each message document. This allows security rules to explicitly verify if the `request.auth.uid` is either the sender or receiver, granting access to the message within its conversation, without requiring a `get()` to the parent `Conversation` document or other user profiles.

**Structural Segregation and QAPs (Queryable Authorization Patterns):**

*   **Publicly Discoverable Collections:** Collections like `/products` and `/auctions` are top-level. This design allows for broad, public `list` operations (QAPs) for discovery, enabling buyers to browse and filter items by category, location, and status. Security rules for these collections can permit `read: true` globally, with `write` access restricted to the item's `sellerId` (which is denormalized within the document).
*   **User-Owned Data:** Private or user-specific data, such as a user's blocked list, follows a path-based ownership model, e.g., `/users/{blockerId}/blockedUsers/{blockedId}`. This makes rules simple: `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == blockerId;` and supports QAPs for listing a user's blocked accounts.
*   **Reviews for Sellers:** Reviews are structured under the seller's profile (`/users/{sellerId}/reviews/{reviewId}`). This design facilitates easy querying of all reviews for a specific seller's profile, while the `reviewerId` within the `Review` document ensures authorization independence for the reviewer to manage their own reviews.
*   **Conversations and Messages:** `Conversations` are top-level to group messages. `conversations/{conversationId}` documents explicitly contain `participantIds`. Messages are then subcollections `/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`. Access to messages relies on `senderId` and `receiverId` being present in each `Message` document, making `list` operations for messages within a conversation robust for participants (QAPs are supported by client-side queries for `senderId` or `receiverId` and rule validation). 

**Global Roles (DBAC):**
*   Administrative roles, if implemented beyond `reporterId` specific to reports, would be handled via dedicated collections (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`) to enable DBAC, allowing rules to check `exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` without using custom claims, as mandated.
